title: Lussa quercifolia
taxonomy: Lussa quercifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae. It is native to the Mediterranean region. Lussa quercifolia is a small tree or shrub that grows up to 10 meters in height. The plant is known for its leaves, which resemble those of oak trees, and its white or yellow flowers. The classification of Lussa quercifolia is still under research, but it is believed to be a member of the Sapindaceae family.
uses: Lussa quercifolia is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Africa. The plant is reported to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. However, more research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using Lussa quercifolia for medicinal purposes. intro:
There is no information available on the plant Lussa quercifolia. However, I can provide information on a plant that is often referred to as Lussa, which is actually a synonym for Lycopus quercifolius. Lycopus quercifolius, also known as Lussa or Quaking Grass, is a perennial herb native to Europe. 

Lycopus quercifolius has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and inflammation, due to its health benefits. The plant's bioactive constituents include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Lycopus quercifolius is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
